pred_reason: The SWC-107 label is appropriate because multiple findings demonstrate reentrancy vulnerabilities, including the onSushiReward function violating Checks-Effects-Interactions and an inadequate lock modifier enabling cross-function reentrancy attacks. Evidence: onSushiReward makes external transfers before updating state (Finding 1); Lock modifier provides insufficient cross-function reentrancy protection (Finding 2); Slither detected both reentrancy-no-eth and cross-function reentrancy warnings

findings.Description: The onSushiReward function violates Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern by making external token transfers before updating state variables. This vulnerability appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Potential theft of rewards through cross-function reentrancy attacks, allowing attackers to manipulate reward calculations and drain funds

findings.Description: The lock modifier provides insufficient protection against cross-function reentrancy, allowing state manipulation through other unprotected functions. This issue appears in the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Cross-function reentrancy attacks can exploit inconsistent state to drain funds or manipulate reward distributions
findings.Location: lock modifier implementation, Slither cross-function reentrancy warnings

findings.Description: The init() function lacks access control and can be called by any user before proper initialization. This vulnerability appears only in the smart contract code. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Attackers can take ownership of the contract and set malicious parameters, leading to complete compromise and fund theft

findings.Issue: Missing access control on updatePool function
findings.Description: The updatePool function is public and lacks onlyMCV2 modifier, allowing unauthorized state manipulation. This issue appears only in the smart contract code. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Unauthorized users can manipulate reward accumulation state, leading to incorrect reward distributions

findings.Description: Integer division truncation in accToken1PerShare accumulation causes systematic precision loss over time. This issue appears only in the smart contract code. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Users receive fewer rewards than entitled, with impact growing over time as truncation errors accumulate

findings.Description: The pending variable is declared but not initialized before use, leading to undefined behavior. This issue appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Location: onSushiReward function (pending variable declaration), Slither uninitialized-local detection

findings.Description: Multiple functions lack zero-address validation for critical parameters. This issue appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Potential loss of funds or contract functionality if zero addresses are used in critical operations
findings.Location: reclaimTokens, transferOwnership, constructor functions; Slither missing-zero-check detection

findings.Description: Multiple functions use block.timestamp for critical comparisons and calculations. This issue appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Potential manipulation through miner influence on block timestamps
findings.Location: onSushiReward, pendingToken, updatePool functions; Slither timestamp detection
